recoveryMedicalDisclaimer.en: Journey Forward is not a medical device and does not diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any medical condition. This timeline is educational and reflects general recovery patterns only. Individual recovery varies. If you drink heavily, have a history of withdrawal, seizures, hallucinations, confusion, or feel physically unsafe, speak with a healthcare professional before stopping suddenly or seek urgent medical care.
recoveryMedicalDisclaimer.desc: Medical/safety disclaimer shown above the healing timeline on the Recovery screen.

recoveryMindLabel.desc: Uppercase section label introducing the psychological/mind notes for a recovery milestone.

recoveryM1Mind.en: You might feel a mix of relief and anxiety as your daily routine shifts. This is the normal friction of change.

recoveryM1Experience.en: The first urges may appear. They can feel urgent, but they are temporary waves.
recoveryM1Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 1 (12 hours) — what you may experience.

recoveryM1Tip.en: Drink a large glass of water. When an urge hits, focus only on getting through the next hour.

recoveryM2Mind.en: Your brain's reward circuitry is noticing the absence of its usual chemical trigger, which can cause irritability or a low mood.

recoveryM2Experience.en: You may feel emotionally raw, tired, or slightly restless.
recoveryM2Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 2 (24 hours) — what you may experience.

recoveryM2Tip.en: Sleep and rest are your best allies right now. Keep your evening routine calm, quiet, and consistent.

recoveryM3Mind.en: Your system is seeking balance. The intensity you feel right now is the feeling of that adjustment taking place.

recoveryM3Experience.en: Restlessness and strong urges are common here. You might feel “wired” or on edge.
recoveryM3Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 3 (48 hours) — what you may experience.

recoveryM3Tip.en: Be especially patient with yourself today. If you experience shaking, confusion, hallucinations, seizures, severe agitation, or feel unsafe, seek urgent medical support.
recoveryM3Tip.desc: Recovery milestone 3 (48 hours) — actionable tip with safety warning.

recoveryM4Mind.en: The mental fog often begins to thin. Neurotransmitter production starts to slowly adjust, paving the way for more natural energy.

recoveryM4Experience.en: A small window of calm may emerge. You might feel a quiet, cautious optimism taking root.
recoveryM4Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 4 (3 days) — what you may experience.

recoveryM4Tip.en: Reaching 72 hours is meaningful. Mark it with comfort, care, and support.

recoveryM5Mind.en: You may notice unusually vivid dreams — some people experience this as their sleep pattern settles into a new rhythm.

recoveryM5Experience.en: Improved clarity, though your mood may still naturally swing up and down.
recoveryM5Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 5 (1 week) — what you may experience.

recoveryM5Tip.en: Anchor yourself in routine. A predictable morning and evening structure is a powerful tool right now.

recoveryM6Mind.en: Concentration and short-term memory often start to feel sharper. Each healthier choice you repeat helps lay down new patterns.

recoveryM6Experience.en: You might start feeling surprisingly well, though random moments of emptiness are still normal.
recoveryM6Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 6 (2 weeks) — what you may experience.

recoveryM6Tip.en: This is when overconfidence can sneak in. Stay connected to your daily practices and support systems.

recoveryM7Mind.en: The brain systems involved in impulse control, decision-making, and emotional regulation may begin to feel steadier over time.

recoveryM7Experience.en: Emotional regulation continues to improve, and building resilience becomes a steady practice.
recoveryM7Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 7 (1 month) — what you may experience.

recoveryM7Tip.en: Review your journey so far. Note the situations that still feel tricky, and plan how you will navigate them gracefully.

recoveryM8Mind.en: For many people, the ability to find genuine satisfaction in simple, everyday activities slowly returns at this stage.

recoveryM8Experience.en: Many people describe feeling more like themselves again. Motivation may feel more available, though it can still rise and fall.
recoveryM8Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 8 (3 months) — what you may experience.

recoveryM8Tip.en: Continue to cultivate your environment. Hobbies, nature, and relationships are deeply protective elements of your growth.

recoveryM9Mind.en: Urges may become less frequent or easier to move through.

recoveryM9Experience.en: The highs and lows of early recovery begin to smooth out into a more consistent rhythm.
recoveryM9Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 9 (6 months) — what you may experience.

recoveryM9Tip.en: Take a moment to honor the quiet days. Peace and stability are among the quiet rewards of this process.

recoveryM10Mind.en: You have lived through many seasons, routines, and emotional moments with more awareness and care.

recoveryM10Experience.en: Support may still matter, and needing it does not diminish your progress.
recoveryM10Experience.desc: Recovery milestone 10 (1 year) — what you may experience.

recoveryM10Tip.en: Reflect on the person you were twelve months ago. Write them a letter from where you stand today.

recoveryM11Mind.en: Recovery may feel less like something you are forcing and more like a way of living you have grown into.
recoveryM11Mind.desc: Recovery milestone 11 (2 years & beyond) — mind/psychological note.

recoveryM11Tip.en: Your story may become a source of comfort for someone else. When the moment feels right, share your strength with someone just beginning their path.
recoveryM11Tip.desc: Recovery milestone 11 (2 years & beyond) — actionable tip.
